preview_text:
The model rates Argentina as a heavy favorite at 84% to win, leaving Egypt just under 1% chance of victory. The underdog's path runs almost entirely through a draw, which the model assigns 16% probability—a meaningful but secondary outcome for a side facing such a sharp deficit in the forecast.

The win-loss gap is substantial, suggesting Argentina enters as decisively the stronger side by the model's measure. Yet a draw carries genuine plausibility at 16%, indicating the match is not modeled as a coronation; Egypt has room to frustrate and compete for a point, even if a win sits far outside the model's base case.

Model and market diverge notably on Argentina's win probability—the model favors Argentina by roughly 12 percentage points more than sportsbooks do. Polymarket prices track the consensus closely. The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment is correct; the discrepancy may reflect different underlying assumptions about strength or uncertainty. Sportsbooks assign Egypt considerably higher win equity (9%) than the model does (1%), a gap worth noting for bettors evaluating value.
